t_ch: 这件事，我劝你不要说出去。因为每个说出秘密的人，现在都已经消失了……
t_en: Look, you gotta keep this to yourself, because all those who have revealed this secret "got vanished" without a trace...

t_ch: 那你是不是也快消失了？
t_en: Does that mean you are "getting vanished" as well?

t_ch: …只要你别说出去。
t_en: ...I'd be fine as long as you don't tell anyone else.

t_ch: 有的。有一个机密事宜，在神策府知道的人都寥寥可数，我说与你听……
t_en: Yes. This is confidential information that few know in the Seat of Divine Foresight. So, look...

t_ch: 你看门口站岗的那些卫兵，领头的叫浴铁…其实，他们都叫「浴铁」。「浴铁」并非一个人的名字，而是一个组织。
t_en: Those guards over there at the entrance, we call their leader Yutie... But the truth is, they're all Yutie. Yutie is not a person's name, but an organization.

t_ch: 这个组织隐秘而伟大，世代守护神策府的安全。
t_en: This organization is well-hidden and powerful, protecting the safety of the Seat of Divine Foresight for generations.

t_ch: 每天站在那里的浴铁都是不同的人，但你从来都不会发现。因为他们训练严明，每个人都被训练成了一模一样的性格。
t_en: The Yutie standing there changes every day, but you'd never know it. They're all trained to act the same and assume the same personality.

t_ch: 不过你如果不识趣地偷看到一位「浴铁」的面孔，他们就会只剩下一副面孔了。其他的人就会不动声色地消失，就像从未出现过。
t_en: If you catch a glimpse of a Yutie's face, all the others' faces will simply disappear. They just fade away, like they were never even there.

t_ch: 灾难终于结束了…
t_en: The disaster is finally over...

t_ch: 如果没有你的帮助，事情不会这么顺利…但真的结束了吗？
t_en: Without your help, things wouldn't have gone so swimmingly... But, is it truly over?

t_ch: 无论是太卜来代行将军之责，还是我手头积压的工作，这都说明——对于神策府而言，不把最后一个药王残党缉拿归案，这就还称不上是结束。
t_en: The master diviner acting as the general and my backlog of work show that things aren't over for the Seat of Divine Foresight until the very last Disciple of Sanctus Medicus is arrested.

t_ch: 景元为罗浮受了那么重的伤，我光是看着就觉得心如刀绞。所以，我也希望尽自己的努力，还给他一个干干净净的仙舟罗浮。
t_en: It breaks my heart to see Jing Yuan suffering such a serious injury for the Luofu. So, I hope to do my best to present him with a clean Xianzhou Luofu.

t_ch: 无论如何，请允许我代表我个人对你说一句——非常，非常感谢你。
t_en: Anyway, please allow me to say this personally: Thank you very, very much.

t_ch: 哎…今日府内倒也依旧风平浪静……
t_en: *sigh* ...What a quiet day at the Seat of Divine Foresight today...

t_ch: 你在自言自语吗？
t_en: Are you talking to yourself?

t_ch: 没、没有！只是有些困倦，不曾想竟还胡言乱语起来了，望将军莫见怪……
t_en: N—No, I'm not! I was feeling a little tired, and was just muttering to myself. Please don't penalize me, General...

t_ch: 风平浪静不好吗？
t_en: Isn't it good to be quiet at work?

t_ch: 当然好啊。只是长久不见波澜，难免教人觉得乏味……
t_en: Sure, it's great. But it's starting to get a little boring at work when nothing ever happens...

t_ch: 咦？这不是前日随云骑军一道缉凶的{NICKNAME}大人嘛！
t_en: Hmm? Well if it isn't {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}, back from hunting criminals with the Cloud Knights!

t_ch: 卑职是神策府与景元将军的侍卫总长。久仰大名，今日幸会！
t_en: I've been humbly appointed as chief guard for the Seat of Divine Foresight and General Jing Yuan, and I've heard much about your pursuits. Pleasure to make your acquaintance!

t_ch: 不知您此番光临神策府所为何事？府内同僚大多公务繁忙，招待不周，还请见谅！
t_en: What affairs bring you to the Seat of Divine Foresight today? Please excuse us for our inadequate hosting formalities, we are currently busy with official matters.

t_ch: 能聊聊天吗？
t_en: Can we chat for a bit?

t_ch: 虽然卑职尚在执勤，但聊聊也无妨。您想聊些什么？
t_en: Despite the fact I'm still on duty, a little chat should be alright. What would you like to chat about?

t_ch: 你居然能在执勤的时候聊天？
t_en: Are you allowed to chat while on-duty?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，料到您会这么问了。
t_en: Hahaha, I knew you'd ask that.

t_ch: 卑职虽为神策府侍卫长，但工作倒也算是清闲。毕竟这堂堂神策府由将军亲自镇守，敢打它主意的宵小之徒可是少之又少。
t_en: I may be the chief guard around these parts, but it's actually quite a leisurely role. After all, the general himself is also based in this office so nobody dares to bother us.

t_ch: 而且，您或许有所不知——景元将军他对待我们这些侍卫…可以说是相当宽松。
t_en: Also, you may not know this, but General Jing Yuan... He can be very lenient with the guards.

t_ch: 对将军来说，只要府内事务运转照旧，那便能证明我们已经履职；执勤时偶尔交谈也无伤大雅，将军于是睁一只眼闭一只眼……
t_en: As long as we keep operations running smoothly, then that's proof enough for him that we're fulfilling our duties. So chattering on the job is fine, and the general will generally turn a blind eye to it...

t_ch: …当然，这些都只是卑职瞎猜的。将军为何允许我们这样放肆？我至今也没能想明白。
t_en: ...But of course, this is all just an unfounded guess from me. Why does the general let us get away with all of this? I still haven't figured that part out.

t_ch: 整个罗浮…不，该说是整个仙舟联盟，恐怕都没几个人能洞察将军的心思吧。
t_en: Nobody on the entire Luofu... No, nobody in the entire Xianzhou Alliance can really read the general's mind.

t_ch: 侍卫长是做什么的？
t_en: What do you get up to as the chief guard?

t_ch: 卑职主要负责景元将军的安全，而卑职手下的诸位云骑则负责神策府、乃至整个长乐天安全之守备。
t_en: I'm mainly responsible for General Jing Yuan's personal security, while my Cloud Knights are in charge of public security for the Seat of Divine Foresight and all of Exalting Sanctum.

t_ch: 景元还需要别人保护？
t_en: Does Jing Yuan really need someone else to protect him?

t_ch: 哈哈，别说是您了，卑职也时常有这种想法——将军虽不以武名世，但他毕竟曾是「云上五骁」中的传奇……
t_en: Haha, you're not the only one to say so. I'm always thinking the same. He's not exactly famous for his martial prowess, but he was still one of the legendary High-Cloud Quintet...

t_ch: 唉，我这样的人本事低微，不及将军万一，怎么配奢谈保护将军？不过是神策府中的一件摆设罢了。
t_en: *sigh* I'm just a lowly, unskilled servant with less power than the general's little toe. How could I ever hope to protect him? I may as well be a flowerpot in the Seat of Divine Foresight.

t_ch: 这个疙瘩在我心里积郁多时，想问将军却又怕唐突。但有一天，将军却开口问我，为何每日值守时愁眉不展。
t_en: This used to fester inside of me for quite some time. I meant to ask the general himself about it but was scared I'd offend him. Until one day, the general himself asked me why I always seemed so down at work.

t_ch: 我说出了心里的疑问。将军却笑着问我，是否还记得云骑军的誓词？
t_en: I voiced my doubts. But he simply laughed as he asked me whether or not I remembered the oath of the Cloud Knights.

t_ch: 那还能有忘记的？「誓如云翳障空，卫蔽仙舟，永不落地」，参军时每个人都要复颂的嘛。
t_en: How could I ever forget? "As clouds that cover the heavens will we protect the Xianzhou and soar eternal." Every soldier in the army recites these lines daily.

t_ch: 我念完誓词，将军就道：「独木不可成林，孤剑不可成军。将军也不过是云骑一员而已，仅有片云如何能翼护仙舟？咱们各有所司，浴铁不要妄自菲薄。」
t_en: After I recited the oath, Jing Yuan said "Just as one tree does not a forest make, one sword does not an army form. I'm only one of many in the Cloud Knights. How can one piece of cloud shield the entire Xianzhou? We all have our own role to play. Don't underestimate yourself, Yutie."

t_ch: 卑职听完深自惭愧，又大受鼓舞。将军真不愧为统帅罗浮的人杰啊！
t_en: Hearing these words gave me both great shame and also great inspiration. No wonder the general is a legendary figure of Luofu!

t_ch: 怎么又是你在执勤？
t_en: How come you're on duty again?

t_ch: 神策府作为军机重地，安全护卫的工作是重中之重。我身为侍卫长，自然应当比别的兄弟干的多一些。
t_en: We're an important military department, and our security work is of the utmost priority. As the chief guard, it's only fair that I put in a little more effort than the rest.

t_ch: 等彦卿兄弟再长高一些，他便能穿上合身的盔甲，与我轮流值守了。
t_en: Once Yanqing grows a little taller and fits this armor properly, we'll rotate and take turns guarding.

t_ch: 真期待这一天啊，这小家伙的身手我早就赶不上了，侍卫长的职务恐怕也该早些让贤了。
t_en: I'm looking forward to that day — this kid's surpassed me already, and I should probably be yielding to him my role as the chief guard.

t_ch: 先聊到这里吧。
t_en: We'll end the talk here for now.

t_ch: 那么卑职便继续在此戒备，恕不远送。
t_en: I'll continue my guard duty. Please take care.

t_ch: 不打扰你工作了。
t_en: I'll leave you to your work.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人，又见面了！不知卑职能如何帮到您？
t_en: {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}, we meet again! How am I able to assist you today?

t_ch: 灾难终于结束了…
t_en: The disaster is finally over...

t_ch: 是啊，终于结束了。说起来，卑职和将军大人申请了好几次，要上前线去处理建木危机。
t_en: Yes, it is. Speaking of which, I have asked for the general's permission to deal with the Ambrosial Arbor Crisis at the frontline several times.

t_ch: 可是将军大人一直不肯放卑职走，说找不到能替代卑职站岗的人。就这么拖了好久，直到整件事告一段落，卑职都没去成前线。
t_en: However, he was reluctant to let me go, saying it was hard to find someone to replace me. My request was delayed for a long time, and I did not make it to the front line in the end.

t_ch: 其实卑职心里也清楚，神策府的侍卫长并不是什么难以替代的岗位。将军大人不肯放卑职去前线，只是卑职曾经有过魔阴身风险，担心卑职出危险罢了。
t_en: As a Chief Guard, I know it's not that hard to find my replacement. The general refused to send me to the frontline only because I was at risk of becoming mara-struck, and he was worried about my safety.

t_ch: 后来青镞也劝卑职了，让卑职想清楚，自己万一出了什么事，将军大人一定会很伤心的。卑职觉得她说得在理，也就没再坚持。
t_en: Later on, Qingzu came to advise me. She said that the general would be devastated if anything bad happened to me. Thinking she was right, I did not insist.

t_ch: 唉…可是现在想想那些再也没能回家的将士，卑职也不知道卑职当时到底该不该坚持去前线了……
t_en: But now, thinking of those warriors who couldn't make it back home, I don't even know if I should've insisted going to the frontline...

t_ch: 姐，生物科技这个过不了，抚恤金这个…将军点头了，我也没问题，商业贸易那个你们再等等，你们还没跟商团协商过，对吧？
t_en: Sis, biotech didn't pass, and as for the pension... The general agreed, and I'm fine with it, too. Hit pause on trading for now. You guys haven't negotiated with the trade delegations yet, have you?

t_ch: 诶，通融通融嘛，这点小事，你先去跟将军说好了，我这边也好办事嘛。
t_en: Gosh, let's give them a break then. It's nothing really. You go and talk things through with the general and I'll take care of things from here.

t_ch: 姐，你老是让我「通融通融」，我现在在将军面前根本抬不起头。搞得我们神策府是你们天舶司治下的小办事处一样……
t_en: Sis, you're always telling me to give people a break. I can barely show my face to the general anymore. It's like the Seat of Divine Foresight is just a subordinate office working for the benefit of the Sky-Faring Commission...

t_ch: 海海，话可不能这样说嘛……
t_en: C'mon, don't be like that...

t_ch: 这里是神策府，请注意公序良俗。
t_en: This is the Seat of Divine Foresight. Please watch what you say.

t_ch: 这个…我们俩是姐弟，姐弟说话亲昵一些怎么了嘛？
t_en: Huh... We're siblings. What's wrong with siblings talking more intimately than other people?

t_ch: 姐，人家说得对，我们是在神策府谈公事，别再拿我们的血缘关系套近乎了。
t_en: Sis, they're right. We're talking business here in the Seat of Divine Foresight. Let's not bring our family connection into this.

t_ch: 海海，话可不能这样说嘛…
t_en: You can't say that...

t_ch: 老弟，你怎么沉默了，接着说呀。
t_en: Why aren't you talking, dear brother? Go on.

t_ch: 姐，人家是在提醒咱俩，这是公共场合，说话注意一些分寸。
t_en: Sis, they're reminding us that this is a public place here. Let's be careful about what we say.

t_ch: 这不是景元将军的贵客吗？让您见笑了。这是我老姐永念，隶属于天舶司，我是神策府的永海，请问您今天是有什么要事吗？
t_en: Isn't this one of General Jing Yuan's esteemed guests? Apologies for the ruckus. This is my sister Yong Nian over at the Sky-Faring Commission, and I'm Yong Hai from the Seat of Divine Foresight. To what do we owe the pleasure?

t_ch: 只是想和你们俩闲聊几句。
t_en: I'd like to have a little chat with you both.

t_ch: 好啊，聊啊，顺便你再帮我劝几句。我这小弟根本就不听老姐这边的需求。
t_en: Great, let's chat. You can also help me persuade this guy. My little brother never listens to his older sister's needs.

t_ch: 弟弟有什么需求不答应？
t_en: What do you want that he rejected?

t_ch: 别听我姐胡说，再说了这些公务也不该讲给{NICKNAME}你听。
t_en: Don't listen to her. Besides, this is official business and we shouldn't be talking about it in front of {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 这有什么嘛，就是发明了一种新技术，有海外商团希望就这项技术展开合作，就差神策府这边点头了。
t_en: So what? It's a new technological breakthrough. Some foreign business interests want to invest as a joint venture, and all we need is for the Seat of Divine Foresight to green-light things.

t_ch: 姐，这可不是应该对客人细说的内容。
t_en: Sis, we shouldn't be telling strangers these details.

t_ch: 我明白了，仙舟机密不便打探。
t_en: Got it. Asking about Xianzhou secrets is inappropriate.

t_ch: 感谢您的谅解。
t_en: Thanks for understanding.

t_ch: 你要是不讲，就没有意思了。
t_en: It's no fun if you aren't going to talk a little.

t_ch: 哎，说不过你们俩…我就选些能说的部分吧。
t_en: *sigh* I just can't win against you two... I'll just be careful with what I say then.

t_ch: 我姐要推进的这项技术，叫做「太岁机巧」。名字只是暂定的，因为它其实不是那种会跑来跑去的「机巧」，而是类似玉兆那样的设备。
t_en: My sister wants to promote this new tech called Lucidum Ingenium. The name's not finalized yet because it's not really an autonomous ingenium. It's a small device more like a Jade Abacus.

t_ch: 「太岁」是一种原产于鳞渊境的生物。它结构简单，也没什么真正意义上的智能，但却有一项特殊的本能。
t_en: Lucidums are a type of organism native to the Scalegorge Waterscape. They're devoid of any intelligence with an incredibly simple biostructure, but possess a special natural instinct.

t_ch: 它能在根系与食物之间迅速寻找到最短路径，以最为节约资源的路径加以连接。一些兵法博物学家就意识到，可以利用这种特性制造一种演算系统。
t_en: Lucidums can rapidly find the shortest route between their root system and a nutrient source, optimizing pathing to conserve energy. Some military scientists realized that this could be used to develop a new system of calculations.

t_ch: 总之，只要将改造后的太岁封在一个容器中，只需要不断切换食物的位置，就能不断改变它的连接，使它实现玉兆的功能…当然，计算速度上远不如玉兆。
t_en: A Lucidum captured inside a vessel only needs for a food source to be constantly switching locations for it to update its pathways and produce similar functions to a jade abacus... But at a far, far slower processing speed, naturally.

t_ch: 但是太岁机巧不是玉兆，玉兆阻塞干扰是没办法对太岁机巧奏效的。
t_en: But a Lucidum Ingenium isn't a jade abacus, and jade abaci inhibitors have no effect on Lucidum Ingenia.

t_ch: 有一些商团在黏菌计算机技术上颇有见解，希望和我们达成合作。但是你看到了，神策府对这样的合作过于谨慎了。
t_en: Certain business interests with a keen insight into mycelium processors are looking to cooperate with us. But, as you can see the Seat of Divine Foresight is being overly cautious.

t_ch: 事关核心技术，你们天舶司应该再慎重一些。
t_en: This involves Xianzhou's core technology. It's you guys at the Sky-Faring Commission who should be more cautious.

t_ch: 你们姐弟俩关系太好了吧…
t_en: You two have a great sibling bond between you...

t_ch: 是挺好的，不过已经影响到我的正常工作了，实在没有什么可以说的。
t_en: We do, but it does impact how I do my job. That's all.

t_ch: 怎么没有什么可说的？小时候，这小子特别可爱。我说要做飞行士，他就说他也要做飞行士，「以后陪姐姐一起遨游太空！」
t_en: What do you mean, "That's all"? This punk used to be so cute when we were little. I'd say how I dreamed of being a pilot one day, and he'd say the same, "I want to travel the universe with you, sis!"

t_ch: 快别说了…怪丢人的……
t_en: Please shut up... You're embarrassing me...

t_ch: 这人就骗我。明明说好一起做飞行士，这小子就跑去从军了。现在天天刁难我。
t_en: But this kid tricked me. He said that he'd become a pilot with me, and then abandoned me, joined the army, and revel in contributing to my headaches daily.

t_ch: 老姐你也没当成飞行士啊！你这不是文职人员吗！
t_en: C'mon sis, you never became a pilot either — you're a civil servant!

t_ch: 哎…这么一想，咱们姐俩还真是像啊。
t_en: *sigh* Well, when you put it like that, I guess we are pretty similar after all.

t_ch: 不是一家人不进一家门嘛。不过一码是一码，该走流程的事，我绝不会通融。
t_en: I guess apples don't fall far from the tree after all. These are two separate issues though: When it comes to proper processes, I'll never just give anyone "a break."

t_ch: …话是这么说，但是一家人办事，总得比那些外人方便吧？
t_en: ...Maybe so, but it's always easier for family to come together and do business, right?

t_ch: 哎，你爱怎么说怎么说吧。
t_en: Tsk, whatever you say, sis.

t_ch: 你们继续，我先走了。
t_en: You guys go on, then. I better get out of your hair.

t_ch: 嗯，再见，路上小心。
t_en: So long, and safe travels.

t_ch: 路上小心。
t_en: Safe travels.

t_ch: …长乐天哪有那么多空余的地方…哎？{NICKNAME}大人，您来了啊？
t_en: ...There's nowhere near enough free space in the Exalting Sanctum though... Eh? {NICKNAME}, what are you doing here?

t_ch: 这臭小子不听老姐这边的需求，只会转移话题，{NICKNAME}大人，你说说他呀。
t_en: This punk isn't listening to his sister, and keeps trying to change the subject. {NICKNAME}, can you have a word with him?

t_ch: 灾难过后你们还忙吗？
t_en: The disaster is over, are you guys still busy?

t_ch: 我基本已经搞明白了…我忙不忙根本不取决于什么灾难什么建木什么危机，只取决于我姐。
t_en: I think I've figured it out... It's my sister who decides whether my life is busy, not the Ambrosial Arbor crisis.

t_ch: 哦，你血脉相连的大个活姐姐，对你来说就和建木生发差不多是吧？
t_en: Oh, I see. To you, I'm like the Ambrosial Arbor's new growth, right?

t_ch: 那还是不一样的，建木至少不会每天钻进神策府，拿一些很难通过的企划案找我耗一整天。
t_en: There are some differences. At least the Ambrosial Arbor doesn't visit the Seat of Divine Foresight every single day to pester me with proposals that can hardly be approved.

t_ch: 那没办法，你有你的职责，我也有我的使命，咱俩就每天耗着呗。
t_en: Well, you have your duties, and I have my mission. We have no choice but to be at each other's throat.

t_ch: 唉…怎么说呢，咱姐弟俩就是性格太像了……
t_en: Alas... How should I put it? Our personalities are way too similar...

t_ch: 人们都说，十王好过，书吏难缠～让我看看，今天又有谁阳寿已尽～
t_en: Everyone always says: "The Ten Lords have it easy. It's the scribes that are hard to deal with~" Let's see who has expired their wakespan today~

t_ch: 咦…来者何人？糟糕！难不成是犯人又跑出来了？武弁！武弁在哪里啊？救命啊！又有人越狱了！
t_en: Eek! Who's there? Darn it! Did one of the prisoners get out again? Warden! Where is the warden? Help me! One of the prisoners has escaped again!

t_ch: 我警告你不要靠近我啊！我我我我我可是很凶的！你见没见识过什么叫判官夺命笔？信不信我一笔写死你！
t_en: I'm warning you, don't come any closer! I—I—I... I'm dangerous, you know! Have you ever heard of the Judge's Death Sentence Brush? I'll wipe you out with one brush stroke, just wait and see!

t_ch: 冷静点，我不是犯人。
t_en: Calm down, I am not a prisoner.

t_ch: 不可能！你这恶徒休想欺骗我！这幽囚狱里每个囚犯的脸我都记得！你就是那个…那个那个……
t_en: Impossible! Don't think you can deceive me, villain! I remember the face of every inmate in The Shackling Prison! You're that... Umm, that one...

t_ch: 银河球棒侠的威名听说过没？
t_en: Have you ever heard of the great Galactic Baseballer?

t_ch: 我管你是球棒侠还是扫帚侠，在幽囚狱是龙也得盘着，是虎也得卧着！
t_en: I don't care if you're a galactic baseballer or a galactic janitor! It doesn't matter how powerful you are, The Shackling Prison will have you on your knees!

t_ch: 我记得你这张脸！别提醒我，我能想起来！让我想想你犯了什么事…虽然有点模糊，但我隐约记得你的江湖匪号！叫什么「无命客」来着？
t_en: I remember your face! Don't remind me, let me think... Let me think about what transgression you committed... Thought it's a little blurry, I remember you had a title of some kind. What was it called? The "Lifeless"?

t_ch: 唉哟，真是对不起！我这两天忙着记录各种罪犯的案卷，脑子里都是罪犯的事，闹出乌龙了，您可千万别见怪！
t_en: Agh! I'm so, so sorry! I've been busy these two days recording all kinds of criminal cases. My head's filled with stuff about them, so please don't take this personally!

t_ch: 虽然您是咱们「罗浮」的贵客，但幽囚狱案牍库终归是十王司的机要重地，原则上是不对异邦人开放的。
t_en: Though you are an esteemed guest of the Luofu, but the Shackling Prison's document office is a critical installation for the Ten-Lords Commission. On principle, it should not be open to outworlders.

t_ch: 您若是实在对这案牍库有所好奇…不妨直接问我吧！我在案牍库干了几百年，只要是不那么涉密的内容，我都能满足您的好奇心。
t_en: If you're really curious about the document office... Then why don't you just ask me! I've worked here for centuries. As long as the information isn't classified, I'll be able to satisfy your curiosity.

t_ch: 想问你些事情。
t_en: I would like to ask you some questions.

t_ch: 您请问吧，我尽可能回答。
t_en: Ask away. I'll do my best to answer.

t_ch: 都有哪些问题你不能答？
t_en: Are there any questions you can't answer?

t_ch: 哦哟～我不能答的问题可太多了——要犯的关押位置、武弁的换岗时间、食堂的供货渠道、寒鸦大人的实际年龄…这些都是幽囚狱的最高机密。
t_en: Oh, way too many. I can't tell you where major criminals are held, when the guards get relieved, the supply channels to the cafeteria, Madam Hanya's actual age... All these are the Shackling Prison's most highly classified secrets.

t_ch: 最后一个问题是怎么回事？
t_en: What's that last thing you said? The one about the age?

t_ch: 别问了…别问了…这是碰都不能碰的话题……
t_en: Don't ask... Just don't... These are unmentionable topics...

t_ch: 怎么我感兴趣的全是机密？
t_en: Why is everything I'm interested in confidential?

t_ch: 哈哈，毕竟就是「不该知道的事」才最吸引人呢！
t_en: Hahah, don't you know? "Forbidden knowledge" is always most alluring!

t_ch: 案牍库是做什么的？
t_en: What does the document office do?

t_ch: 案牍库可以说是幽囚狱的信息中枢。幽囚狱里这么多要犯，身份信息可全都存储在咱们这案牍库里呢。
t_en: The document office is the Shackling Prison's central hub. The personal information of all these prisoners in the Shackling Prison are all stored in our document office.

t_ch: 而且案牍库记录的可不止是身份信息——犯人们的生平经历、犯罪事实、口供证词、判决文书、服刑表现，事无巨细，统统都记录在案。
t_en: Plus, we log more than just personal information. Every minute detail is recorded from prisoners' life experiences, criminal records, oral testimonies, judgment documents, and even their performance in serving their sentence.

t_ch: 总而言之，一个人只要进了幽囚狱，就再也没有任何隐私可言。
t_en: In summation, as soon as someone enters The Shackling Prison, they have no more secrets left to hide.

t_ch: 这么多档案，该怎么管理呢？
t_en: How do you manage this many files?

t_ch: 靠成千上万个像我这样细心又负责的书吏来管理咯。整个十王司系统中上百亿份档案，一直都是纯靠人力来管理维护的，所以书吏的编制一度超过了冥差。
t_en: Through tens of thousands of attentive and responsible scribes like me. The Ten-Lords Commission's system holds hundreds of millions of files, and they have always been maintained through manpower. That's why the staffing of scribes exceeded even the spiritfarers for a while.

t_ch: 十王司也确实尝试过开发一套档案管理玉兆来提高了办公效率，但…最后因为种种「客观因素」，这个计划没有推行下去。
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission also tried to develop a file management abacus to improve work efficiency, but... due to various "objective factors," the plan couldn't be enacted.

t_ch: 说是「客观因素」，其实归根结底就是…百万书吏衣食所系。嘿嘿，您懂的。
t_en: Well, we say "objective factors," but the ultimate factor is.... well, these countless files also feed lots of hungry mouths, if you know what I mean, hehe.

t_ch: 档案放一起，不怕被一锅端么？
t_en: All these files together... are you not worried about them being wiped out in an accident?

t_ch: 这您大可放心，这案牍库归根结底只是个远程访问接口，一锅端了也没用。案牍原件全部都存储在某个玉兆阵列中。
t_en: You can rest assured about that. This document office is ultimately just a remote access interface. Even if the office was somehow "eliminated," it wouldn't have any effect. All the original documentation are stored on a certain jade abacus matrix somewhere.

t_ch: 至于那个存储案牍的玉兆阵列在哪里…我也不知道。我怀疑压根不在「罗浮」，甚至不在仙舟联盟。
t_en: As for where the jade abacus array that holds all the documents is stored... Well, I don't know. I suspect it isn't even on the Luofu, or even the Xianzhou Alliance at all.

t_ch: 内部甚至还有传言说，玉兆阵列藏在塔拉萨的某个海沟里！当然，这就不太现实了，当个故事听就好。
t_en: There are even internal rumors that the matrix is hidden in one of Thalassa's ocean trenches! Of course, that's not very realistic, so just treat it as a story.

t_ch: 这工作干这么久不枯燥么？
t_en: Do you not find it boring and monotonous, having worked here so long?

t_ch: 其实也没那么枯燥。毕竟这儿有堆积如山的档案可以看，随便一本就记录了某个大案要犯的一生，可刺激了！
t_en: It really isn't that boring at all. After all, there is a mountain of files here to read. I can take a flick through a file that records the entire life of a major criminal as I please. It's pretty thrilling!

t_ch: 但要说枯燥，那也是挺枯燥的。幽囚狱这个地方贼古怪，也不知道是不是因为与世隔绝的原因，在这里待久了会觉得时间过得很快。
t_en: But if we're talking monotonous, yeah. The Shackling Prison is an extremely strange place. I'm not sure if it is due to the separation from the real world, but once you've been here for a long time, it feels like no time has passed at all.

t_ch: 总感觉自己昨天还在阳间蹉跎人生，怎么一眨眼就在这幽囚深狱工作几百年了呢？
t_en: I feel like I was still living in the mortal realm just yesterday. How it is that hundred of years can pass by in a blink while I work here?

t_ch: 唉！枯燥不枯燥都无所谓啦！我跟您不一样——还有大好的人生等待您去挥霍，还有数不尽的传奇等待您去书写……
t_en: *sigh* Boring or not, it doesn't matter! I'm not like you... You still have a whole life waiting for you to spend and experience, and countless more legendary exploits that await your accomplishing...

t_ch: 我嘛…从进入十王司开始，人生就结束了。现在这书吏的身份，只不过是一个永无止境的结局罢了。
t_en: As for me... Ever since I joined the Ten-Lords Commission, the curtains have essentially already come down on my life. My current role as scribe is but an "ending" that simply stretches on forever instead of ceasing to exist.

t_ch: 暂时没别的问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 那大人您请自便，有需要再喊我。
t_en: Then please feel free to look around, {M#sir}{F#ma'am}. Call me if you need anything.

t_ch: 我自己逛逛吧。
t_en: I'll look around for myself.

t_ch: 那大人您请自便，有需要再喊我。
t_en: Then please feel free to look around, {M#sir}{F#ma'am}. Call me if you need anything.

t_ch: 人们都说，十王好过，书吏难缠～让我看看，今天又有谁阳寿已尽～
t_en: Everyone always says: "The Ten Lords have it easy. It's the scribes that are hard to deal with~" Let's see who has expired their wakespan today~

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人您又来了啊，今天是有什么事要办吗？
t_en: Esteemed {NICKNAME}! You're back again! Do you have any business you wish to resolve today?

t_ch: 哎…现在的这帮年轻人啊…孺～子～不～可～教～
t_en: *sigh* Youths these days... Simply un-teach-a-ble.

t_ch: 先生好兴致。
t_en: You're in an excellent mood today.

t_ch: 好什么兴致啊，老夫都快烦死了！
t_en: Yes, an excellently annoyed mood!

t_ch: 年轻人怎么你了？
t_en: How have the youths wronged you this time?

t_ch: 嗐…说得又不是你，你跟着起什么急啊……
t_en: Oh, I didn't mean you... What are you getting riled up for...

t_ch: 哎…现在的年轻人啊…
t_en: Hmph... Kids today...

t_ch: 这孩子…你自己不也是年轻人？年轻人别学老人说话，老气横秋的。
t_en: ...Aren't you a youth, yourself? Kids shouldn't talk like their elders, it makes you look old.

t_ch: 仔细一看，这不是{NICKNAME}吗？上次见面，老夫还多亏你搭救呢。
t_en: Hey, aren't you {NICKNAME}? I'm indebted to you for your help the last time we met.

t_ch: 你和我教的那群年轻人不一样的，你有活力，你会去尽可能完成那些你该完成的东西，因为你知道老之将至……
t_en: You're different from the kids I teach. You have energy, and you put all your effort into completing your goals because you understand that age has a way of creeping up on you...

t_ch: 算啦！不跟你抱怨啦！有什么事要找老夫吗？
t_en: Forget it. I'm not going to vent my frustrations out on you! Anything I can help you with today?

t_ch: 想了解工造司…
t_en: Tell me about the Artisanship Commission...

t_ch: 呼呼哈哈哈哈哈…你问吧！老夫不怕你问，只怕答了你也听不懂啊。
t_en: Bwahahaha... Ask away! I'm not afraid of your questions, but I'm afraid you might not understand my answers.

t_ch: 你平时都做什么？
t_en: What do you usually get up to around here?

t_ch: 老夫号称「镕金铄铁第一巧手」，这名号可不是自己吹出来的。那日常的工作，自然就是镕金铄铁。
t_en: They call me "the Smelter Extraordinaire," but I didn't come up with that nickname. As for our day-to-day work, we forge metals here.

t_ch: 不过，身为工正，老夫并不总是需要做一线工作。更多的时间还是用来研发各种家用机巧、改进各种工造设计，诸如此类。
t_en: However, I, as an artisan, am not really involved in front-line work all the time. I put most of my efforts into developing various household ingenia and improving our artifex designs.

t_ch: 你应该也看出来了，仙舟上很多日常物品都有着精巧繁复的外观…提灯上有栩栩如生的鱼，铃铛上有精致可爱的小狮子，无人机是鸟类和瑞兽……
t_en: You've probably noticed the intricate and complex designs for all the household appliances on Xianzhou... Lifelike fish suspended into a lamp, exquisite lions carved into doorbells, drones designed to look like birds or mythical beasts...

t_ch: 这些东西并不会增加物件的功能，但我们还是不遗余力地将它细化。以前还有个化外民说，我们这叫什么…「极繁主义」……
t_en: None of this improves the essential function of these tools, but we still take great pains to craft them well. Another Outworlder once told me that you call this style "Maximalist"?

t_ch: 而设计和迭代这些「极繁主义」的外观，可能已经耗费了老夫数百年的时光。
t_en: Designing and iterating all of these different maximalist looks has already taken up hundreds of years of my life.

t_ch: 很久以后老夫才理解，为什么那位化外民会那么惊讶。因为他们…或者说你们，和我们对物件的「实用性」有着不同的定义。
t_en: It took me an age to realize why that Outworlder was so surprised. Because they all — I mean, you all have a different definition of what "practicality" means.

t_ch: 在我们工造司老手艺人眼里，物件的精致与华美，恰恰是它「实用性」的一环。老夫反倒觉得你们那些光秃秃的「手电」和「旅行箱」缺乏很多「实用性」哩！
t_en: To us Artisanship Commission veterans, intricacy and detail are just one part of an object's practicality. On the contrary, I just so happen to find the sparseness of your "flashlight" and "suitcase" technology to be rather impractical!

t_ch: 这种认知的差异，对老夫来说是千年来的常识，太过理所当然以至于很难跟你解释…但老夫觉得，这是因为我们活得太长了。
t_en: This cognitive difference is only natural from our long-life species' perspective, and of course it's simply impossible for me to explain all this to you... But I think it's actually because we have been living for far too long now.

t_ch: 我们不能容忍无趣无味的东西伴随在身边成百上千年，所以我们关注事物的外表，正如我们重视事物的内里。
t_en: When you live for hundreds of years, you grow intolerant of boring, lackluster objects. And so we pay great attention to how things look, and value the outside appearance of things just as much as the inner functionality.

t_ch: 老夫是不是又说了些难懂的话？哈哈哈哈，罢～了～
t_en: Have I prattled on again about my usual cryptic rants? Heh, whoops~

t_ch: 关于工造司的职责…
t_en: About the job scope at the Artisanship Commission...

t_ch: 工造司的职责啊…这个倒是好解释。工造司就是负责设计、制造和改进仙舟上几乎一切物件的司部。
t_en: Our job scope? Hmm... Now that's far easier to explain! The Artisanship Commission is responsible for the design, manufacturing, and improvement of almost every single item there is aboard the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 小到老夫负责的民用机巧，大到贯星斗舰和洞天，都是工造司制造的…有道是：人之巧，乃可与造化者同功乎！
t_en: It could be something as small as civilian ingenia, of which I am in charge, or something as large as the Sagittarius Starfighter or a Delve... We do it all. As the saying goes: Human ingenuity is as limitless as the heavens!

t_ch: 关于你的学生…
t_en: What about your apprentices...

t_ch: 提起这个老夫就头疼啊！
t_en: I can feel my headache returning already!

t_ch: 你知道为什么工造司以仙舟人为主吗？因为持明和狐族活得都不如仙舟人长。
t_en: Do you know why the Artisanship Commission is predominated by Xianzhou people? It's because the Vidyadhara and the foxians don't live as long as we do.

t_ch: 技术的革新需要身后的积累，而仙舟人最擅长的就是「积累」。
t_en: Technological breakthroughs require the mass accumulation of knowledge, and if there's one thing the Xianzhou people are good at, it's accumulation.

t_ch: 老夫的爱徒们啊，一个个都积累了数百年的学养与经验，却没有半个人有成才的迹象！你说说，这么枯活千年，如野马过荒原，又有何益呢？
t_en: As for my darling apprentices, they've all racked up centuries of education and experience, but not a single one of them have shown any signs of success whatsoever! They're as useless as a chocolate teapot.

t_ch: 其实老夫也想通了。至少在工造领域，仙舟人成也在长生，败也在长生。
t_en: I think I've figured this out now, though. At least as far as the field of artifex is concerned, being long-lived is both an advantage and a downfall of the Xianzhou people.

t_ch: 域外有一世界，为了遵循遍智天君的道路，燃烧生命以追求智慧。他们一个个都聪明绝顶，却也都只能活十几年。
t_en: There's another world out there where they self-immolate in the pursuit of knowledge by following the path of the Wisdomwalker. They represent the highest classes of intellect imaginable, but only live for a dozen years or so.

t_ch: 老夫见过他们的工正，十几岁的少年，聪明绝顶、形容枯槁、命不久矣。他来罗浮向老夫请教，老夫倾囊相授。
t_en: I've seen their artisan before: A mere teenager, haggard beyond his years but frighteningly intelligent — and close to death. He came aboard the Luofu seeking my tutelage, and I gave him my undivided attention.

t_ch: 那位工正的技术远不如老夫，知识和经验更是有限，但他有着惊人的行动力、非凡的想象力和绝佳的设计直觉……
t_en: That artisan was far below my level, and even worse when it came to knowledge and experience, but he possessed a terrifying ambition, incredible imagination, and a top-notch intuition for design...

t_ch: 他白天和老夫学习，晚上疯狂地将白天学到的和想到的记录下来…老夫听说，他在回故乡的路上就死了。
t_en: He'd study with me during the day, then by night he'd frantically record notes about everything he'd learned and thought throughout the day... I later heard that he'd died on his way back home.

t_ch: 但他记录下的一切，足以在他的故乡掀起一场技术革新。
t_en: But all of his notes were enough to trigger a technological revolution back in his homelands.

t_ch: 老夫的爱徒们啊！活得太长了！一切都可以拖到明天，因为还有无数个明天！每个人都像一块湿柴火，怎么点也点不着，不会燃烧，只会冒着白烟散发废气！
t_en: But my darling apprentices! They live far too long! Everything can just be put off until tomorrow, because the tomorrows seemingly never end! They're like wet firewood: They don't burn enough, and even if they catch fire, they just pump out a whole load of useless smoke!

t_ch: 要知道，老之将至啊！仙舟人也不例外。只是这衰老的过程被拉长到难以感知的境地，以至于他们都觉得自己永远也不会老。
t_en: As you know, age has a way of catching up with people! The Xianzhou people are not immune either. It's just that our aging process is stretched out to an impossibly long duration, we feel like we're never getting older.

t_ch: 老夫有时候会暗地里想啊…如果他们都只能活十几岁，那如今的工造司一定是另一番景象了吧。
t_en: Sometimes, I even wonder... If we only lived to be teenagers, what kind of Artisanship Commission would we end up with?

t_ch: 没别的事了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 老夫也要继续忙了。路上小心，下次见。
t_en: It's back to the grind for me. Take care out there, and see you next time.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}小友，又来工造司了？今天有什么事吗？
t_en: Ah, if it isn't young {NICKNAME}! What are you doing back here at the Artisanship Commission?

t_ch: 想问你些事情。
t_en: I would like to ask you some questions.

t_ch: 大人您问。
t_en: Please ask away, {M#sir}{F#ma'am}.

t_ch: 您请问吧，我尽可能回答。
t_en: Ask away. I'll do my best to answer.

t_ch: 都有哪些问题你不能答？
t_en: Are there any questions you can't answer?

t_ch: 哦哟～我不能答的问题可太多了——要犯的关押位置、武弁的换岗时间、食堂的供货渠道、寒鸦大人的实际年龄…这些都是幽囚狱的最高机密。
t_en: Oh, way too many. I can't tell you where major criminals are held, when the guards get relieved, the supply channels to the cafeteria, Madam Hanya's actual age... All these are the Shackling Prison's most highly classified secrets.

t_ch: 最后一个问题是怎么回事？
t_en: What's that last thing you said? The one about the age?

t_ch: 别问了…别问了…这是碰都不能碰的话题……
t_en: Don't ask... Just don't... These are unmentionable topics...

t_ch: 怎么我感兴趣的全是机密？
t_en: Why is everything I'm interested in confidential?

t_ch: 哈哈，毕竟就是「不该知道的事」才最吸引人呢！
t_en: Hahah, don't you know? "Forbidden knowledge" is always most alluring!

t_ch: 案牍库是做什么的？
t_en: What does the document office do?

t_ch: 案牍库可以说是幽囚狱的信息中枢。幽囚狱里这么多要犯，身份信息可全都存储在咱们这案牍库里呢。
t_en: The document office is the Shackling Prison's central hub. The personal information of all these prisoners in the Shackling Prison are all stored in our document office.

t_ch: 而且案牍库记录的可不止是身份信息——犯人们的生平经历、犯罪事实、口供证词、判决文书、服刑表现，事无巨细，统统都记录在案。
t_en: Plus, we log more than just personal information. Every minute detail is recorded from prisoners' life experiences, criminal records, oral testimonies, judgment documents, and even their performance in serving their sentence.

t_ch: 总而言之，一个人只要进了幽囚狱，就再也没有任何隐私可言。
t_en: In summation, as soon as someone enters The Shackling Prison, they have no more secrets left to hide.

t_ch: 这么多档案，该怎么管理呢？
t_en: How do you manage this many files?

t_ch: 靠成千上万个像我这样细心又负责的书吏来管理咯。整个十王司系统中上百亿份档案，一直都是纯靠人力来管理维护的，所以书吏的编制一度超过了冥差。
t_en: Through tens of thousands of attentive and responsible scribes like me. The Ten-Lords Commission's system holds hundreds of millions of files, and they have always been maintained through manpower. That's why the staffing of scribes exceeded even the spiritfarers for a while.

t_ch: 十王司也确实尝试过开发一套档案管理玉兆来提高了办公效率，但…最后因为种种「客观因素」，这个计划没有推行下去。
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission also tried to develop a file management abacus to improve work efficiency, but... due to various "objective factors," the plan couldn't be enacted.

t_ch: 说是「客观因素」，其实归根结底就是…百万书吏衣食所系。嘿嘿，您懂的。
t_en: Well, we say "objective factors," but the ultimate factor is.... well, these countless files also feed lots of hungry mouths, if you know what I mean, hehe.

t_ch: 档案放一起，不怕被一锅端么？
t_en: All these files together... are you not worried about them being wiped out in an accident?

t_ch: 这您大可放心，这案牍库归根结底只是个远程访问接口，一锅端了也没用。案牍原件全部都存储在某个玉兆阵列中。
t_en: You can rest assured about that. This document office is ultimately just a remote access interface. Even if the office was somehow "eliminated," it wouldn't have any effect. All the original documentation are stored on a certain jade abacus matrix somewhere.

t_ch: 至于那个存储案牍的玉兆阵列在哪里…我也不知道。我怀疑压根不在「罗浮」，甚至不在仙舟联盟。
t_en: As for where the jade abacus array that holds all the documents is stored... Well, I don't know. I suspect it isn't even on the Luofu, or even the Xianzhou Alliance at all.

t_ch: 内部甚至还有传言说，玉兆阵列藏在塔拉萨的某个海沟里！当然，这就不太现实了，当个故事听就好。
t_en: There are even internal rumors that the matrix is hidden in one of Thalassa's ocean trenches! Of course, that's not very realistic, so just treat it as a story.

t_ch: 这工作干这么久不枯燥么？
t_en: Do you not find it boring and monotonous, having worked here so long?

t_ch: 其实也没那么枯燥。毕竟这儿有堆积如山的档案可以看，随便一本就记录了某个大案要犯的一生，可刺激了！
t_en: It really isn't that boring at all. After all, there is a mountain of files here to read. I can take a flick through a file that records the entire life of a major criminal as I please. It's pretty thrilling!

t_ch: 但要说枯燥，那也是挺枯燥的。幽囚狱这个地方贼古怪，也不知道是不是因为与世隔绝的原因，在这里待久了会觉得时间过得很快。
t_en: But if we're talking monotonous, yeah. The Shackling Prison is an extremely strange place. I'm not sure if it is due to the separation from the real world, but once you've been here for a long time, it feels like no time has passed at all.

t_ch: 总感觉自己昨天还在阳间蹉跎人生，怎么一眨眼就在这幽囚深狱工作几百年了呢？
t_en: I feel like I was still living in the mortal realm just yesterday. How it is that hundred of years can pass by in a blink while I work here?

t_ch: 唉！枯燥不枯燥都无所谓啦！我跟您不一样——还有大好的人生等待您去挥霍，还有数不尽的传奇等待您去书写……
t_en: *sigh* Boring or not, it doesn't matter! I'm not like you... You still have a whole life waiting for you to spend and experience, and countless more legendary exploits that await your accomplishing...

t_ch: 我嘛…从进入十王司开始，人生就结束了。现在这书吏的身份，只不过是一个永无止境的结局罢了。
t_en: As for me... Ever since I joined the Ten-Lords Commission, the curtains have essentially already come down on my life. My current role as scribe is but an "ending" that simply stretches on forever instead of ceasing to exist.

t_ch: 暂时没别的问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 那大人您请自便，有需要再喊我。
t_en: Then please feel free to look around, {M#sir}{F#ma'am}. Call me if you need anything.

t_ch: 我自己逛逛吧。
t_en: I'll look around for myself.

t_ch: 那大人您请自便，有需要再喊我。
t_en: Then please feel free to look around, {M#sir}{F#ma'am}. Call me if you need anything.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你好。我是太卜司卜者，叫做绘星。负责管理「大衍穷观阵」。
t_en: Hello, {NICKNAME}, I'm Huixing, a diviner at the Divination Commission. I manage the Matrix of Prescience Ultima.

t_ch: 小孩来管理穷观阵？
t_en: They put a child in charge of the Matrix of Prescience?

t_ch: 我岁数比你大。
t_en: I'm older than you.

t_ch: 你一个人负责管理？
t_en: Are you the only person in charge here?

t_ch: 当然不是。但我是总负责人。
t_en: Of course not. But I'm the director.

t_ch: 太卜说了，你是罗浮的贵客，要尽量满足你的要求。所以有任何需要都可以找我。
t_en: The master diviner tells me that you are an esteemed guest of the Luofu, and that we should do our best to comply with your requests. So if there's anything I can do for you, feel free to look for me.

t_ch: 今天有什么需要吗？
t_en: What can I do for you today?

t_ch: 想了解太卜司…
t_en: Tell me about the Divination Commission...

t_ch: 好。我们尽可能长话短说吧，事情挺多的。
t_en: Alright. I'll try to keep things brief, as we do lots of different things here.

t_ch: 太卜司都做什么？
t_en: What does the Divination Commission do?

t_ch: 计算。
t_en: Calculations.

t_ch: …你不喜欢这个答案。
t_en: ...My response has left you dissatisfied.

t_ch: 罗浮航行时，我们计算罗浮的航线，避开凶危的路径。罗浮停泊时，我们计算周遭星体的动向。
t_en: We calculate the Luofu's trajectory as it sails and help it avoid any dangerous routes. When moored, we calculate and track the movements of the celestial bodies around us.

t_ch: 恒星改变温度、引力带来潮汐，「洞天」似乎将我们隔绝于宇宙之外，但宇宙始终对我们施加着影响。
t_en: Changes in star temperature, fluctuations in gravity waves... The delves seemingly isolate us from the rest of the universe, but our fates are still impacted by the expanse.

t_ch: 太卜司最重要的日常工作，就是观察这些环境变化，收集信息，计算事件发生的概率，提出应对之策。
t_en: The Divination Commission's most important day-to-day work is observing these changing phenomena, gathering information, calculating the probabilities of certain occurrences, and proposing contingencies.

t_ch: 这个答案你总能满意了吧。
t_en: Has the answer sated your curiosity?

t_ch: 听说穷观阵能预测未来？
t_en: Is it true that the Matrix of Prescience can predict the future?

t_ch: 确实。
t_en: Affirmative.

t_ch: …我猜你想知道更详细的内容？抱歉，技术细节不能透露。但不妨说个故事，满足你的好奇心。
t_en: ...I guess you'd like to know more details? Sorry, the technical details are all confidential. All I can say is a story to sate your curiosity.

t_ch: 仙舟曾去过一个世界，那里的人用一种叫「水镜」的东西，对未来洞若观火，竟比穷观阵更要精准，我们考察团不由得花功夫研究了一番。
t_en: Xianzhou had once visited a world where the residents used something called an Aquascope to scry into the future. That thing was even more accurate than the Matrix of Prescience, and our exploration team couldn't help but pore ourselves into researching this technology.

t_ch: 那东西远比穷观阵要精准，我们考察团不由得花功夫研究了一番。
t_en: That thing was calibrated even more accurately than the Matrix of Prescience, and we couldn't help but pore ourselves into researching this technology.

t_ch: 后来发现，「水镜」并没有那么神奇。只是因为那世界信息有限，故而预测未来需要的情报量和计算量都相当小。
t_en: We later came to realize that this Aquascope was no mystery at all. That world simply has a limited amount of information. There wasn't much computational power and data input needed to predict the future there.

t_ch: 相比之下，罗浮处于浩瀚星海中，这可不是一个钟表般严密精巧的机械宇宙。
t_en: In comparison, the Luofu sails through galaxies of stars, not a machine-esque world as predictable as a complex clockwork of unerring precision.

t_ch: 所以…穷观阵能否预测未来？能。准确吗？以太卜司每日巨量的占算次数来说，还不够准。
t_en: And so... Can the Matrix of Prescience divine the future? Yes. Is it accurate? Based on the voluminous calculations run daily by the Divination Commission: Not accurate enough.

t_ch: 可能有点答非所问，但我也只能说这么多了。
t_en: Perhaps I strayed off-topic a bit there, but that's really all I can say.

t_ch: 没别的事了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 好的。再见。
t_en: Alright. So long.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你好。今天有什么需要吗？
t_en: Hello, {NICKNAME}. How can I help you today?

t_ch: 静斋最近还好吗？
t_en: How is Jingzhai doing these days?

t_ch: 静斋卜官啊？你为什么要打听这个？
t_en: Jingzhai, our Managing Diviner? Why do you ask?

t_ch: 静斋卜官最近和以前一样，工作认真、做事井井有条的，感觉挺好的呀。
t_en: As usual, Jingzhai is dedicated to her work and very organized. I think she's doing quite well.

t_ch: 最近太卜司的担子也重，常常要加班，她都是主动揽在自己身上，也没见她抱怨过。人品和工作态度都非常棒，是非常出色的工作伙伴。
t_en: Things at the Divination Commission have been hectic lately, and we often have to work overtime. Jingzhai always does the extra work without complaining. She has a great personality and excels at what she does. She is an amazing colleague.

t_ch: 稍等一下。
t_en: One second.

t_ch: 好了。我是地衡司的负责庶务相关内容的净砚，请问有什么可以帮助你的吗？
t_en: Alright. I'm Jingyan from the Realm-Keeping Commission, and I'm in charge of general affairs here. Is there anything that I can help you with?

t_ch: 有什么可以帮助我的？
t_en: What can you help me with?

t_ch: …本来是我在问你，你反倒过来问我了，好吧。
t_en: ...I was just asking you that, but you asked me the same thing. Okay.

t_ch: 仙舟本地人的户籍管理、登记以及维护；案件办理，打架斗殴、持械伤人、偷窃抢劫等等；基础信息采集、人口统计、资源配置……
t_en: Household registrations, applications, and maintenance for local Xianzhou citizens. Handling of cases such as fighting, wounding with a weapon, theft, robbery. Processing census data, demographic statistics, resource allocation...

t_ch: 师傅，不要再念经了。
t_en: I get it. Please stop this monotonous chanting.

t_ch: 看来您对地衡司执事的工作也不是真的很感兴趣。
t_en: Looks like you aren't really that interested in the work of Realm-Keeping Commission officers.

t_ch: 不错，你们的工作做得很好。
t_en: Yes, you've all done a great job.

t_ch: 啊，原来你是来视察工作的吗？感谢您百忙之中莅临地衡司公廨。
t_en: Ah, so you're here to inspect our work then? Thank you for taking the time to visit our humble Realm-Keeping Commission Chancery.

t_ch: 不过现在还是上班时间，请不要耽误其他人正常咨询。
t_en: But I'm afraid it's currently during business hours, so please do not interrupt our everyday consultations.

t_ch: 我遇到赛博诈骗了！
t_en: I've been cyber-scammed!

t_ch: 赛博诈骗？我们这里不用将赛博诈骗和传统诈骗分得那么仔细。
t_en: Cyber-scammed? We don't differentiate between scams happening online or offline here.

t_ch: 请登记一下你的联系方式、诈骗人与你是如何联系的，诈骗经过是怎样的，诈骗数额是怎样的？你先去填表，我帮你验证账户。
t_en: Please register your contact details, how the scammer contacted you, how you were scammed, and how much you were scammed out of? Go fill this out, and I'll verify your account for you.

t_ch: 其实我没有被诈骗。
t_en: Actually, I wasn't scammed at all.

t_ch: 嗯。现在还是上班时间，请不要给执事的正常工作造成麻烦。
t_en: Hmm. It's still currently during business hours, so please do not cause any trouble for our busy officers.

t_ch: 凭我的工作经验来看，您确实也不像是上当受骗的受害者。
t_en: Based on my experience, you don't really look like a victim of scamming.

t_ch: 账、账户上没有交易记录。
t_en: Th—There's no transaction record on the account.

t_ch: 这就有点麻烦了，就算是地衡司也找不回您「没被骗走的钱」。
t_en: This is a bit of a hassle. Not even the Realm-Keeping Commission can get back your "money that wasn't scammed."

t_ch: 这部分只能靠您自己多加努力了，地衡司爱莫能助。
t_en: Seems like you'll have to sort that part out on your own. Our hands are tied here at the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 好像没有庶务相关的事情。
t_en: I don't think I have anything that's connected to general affairs.

t_ch: 请问你还有什么别的事吗？
t_en: Excuse me, do you have anything else?

t_ch: 有些事情想要咨询一下。
t_en: There are a few things I'd like to ask.

t_ch: 嗯，您请讲，我会尽力回答。
t_en: Mmm, go ahead. I'll do my best to answer.

t_ch: 整个公廨好像只有你最靠谱。
t_en: It seems to me that you're the most reliable person in the whole Chancery.

t_ch: 嗯…我不是很方便立刻回答这个问题。请问您是想听地衡司执事的正式回复，还是普通上班族的抱怨？
t_en: Mmm... I'm not at liberty to promptly respond to that question. May I ask, would you like to hear the formal response from a Realm-Keeping Commission officer, or the complaint from a regular staff member?

t_ch: 地衡司执事的正式回复。
t_en: The formal response from a Realm-Keeping Commission officer.

t_ch: 地衡司公廨的所有公职人员都有各自的工作侧重点，每个人都是地衡司不可或缺的组成部分。
t_en: All public officials at the Chancery have their own focus, and everyone is an integral member of the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 我对您「最靠谱」的描述仅表示诚挚的感谢，其他部分不予评价。希望您能继续对地衡司工作保持关注和支持。
t_en: I can only express my gratitude for your assessment as "most reliable," and would refrain from any comments on the other parts. Hopefully you can keep paying attention to and supporting the work of the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 普通上班族的抱怨。
t_en: The complaint from a regular staff member.

t_ch: 是的，你的观察没有错。如果你与两个甩手掌柜一起共事，很快你也能成为「最靠谱的人」。
t_en: Yeah, your observation is not wrong. If you get lumped in with two other lazybones at work, you'll soon become the "most reliable" around the workplace, too.

t_ch: 这可不是什么光荣的称谓，我实在是高兴不起来。
t_en: It isn't a glorious title, so I simply couldn't be happy about it.

t_ch: 听说大毫执事官之前是云骑军？
t_en: I heard the official Dahao used to be in the Cloud Knights?

t_ch: 嗯…这件事有关大毫大人的个人履历。请问您是想听到地衡司执事的正式回复，还是我个人的小道消息？
t_en: Mm-hmm... That's about Official Dahao's personal life. May I ask, would you like to hear the formal response from a Realm-Keeping Commission officer, or a personal gossip from me?

t_ch: 地衡司执事的正式回复。
t_en: The formal response from a Realm-Keeping Commission officer.

t_ch: 大毫执事官曾经在云骑军中的军事经历赋予了他独一无二的责任感。战场的直觉让他在处理地衡司的大事小情时能够有的放矢。
t_en: Official Dahao's military experience in the Cloud Knights gives him a unique sense of responsibility. His intuition on the battlefield grants him a definite sense of purpose when dealing with issues big and small at the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 请您相信地衡司的专业性。执事们拥有不同的履历，能够丰富我们处理复杂社情民情时的经验和手段。噗——
t_en: Please trust in the professionalism of the Realm-Keeping Commission. Our officers come from different backgrounds, which only enriches our experiences and methods of dealing with complex social and public interactions. Pfft—

t_ch: 你在笑什么？
t_en: What are you laughing at?

t_ch: 我们所有地衡司执事都受过严格的训练，无论多好笑，我们都不会笑，除非忍不住。
t_en: We've all undergone strict training at the Realm-Keeping Commission. No matter how funny something is, we will never laugh, unless we can't.

t_ch: 你是不是自己也不信？
t_en: Are you doubting it yourself?

t_ch: 我相信每一位地衡司执事的专业素养，尤其是咱们长乐天公廨，噗——
t_en: I believe in the professionalism of every officer at the Realm-Keeping Commission, especially here in the Exalting Sanctum Chancery. Pfft—

t_ch: 对不起，我想起了一些好笑的事情，噗嗤——
t_en: I'm sorry. I just thought of something funny. Tfft pfft—

t_ch: 个人的小道消息。
t_en: A personal gossip from you.

t_ch: 嗯…我见你不是一个会到处乱讲的大嘴巴，我才告诉你的……
t_en: Hmmm... You don't seem like the gossiping kind, so I'll tell you...

t_ch: 其实大毫大人以前还在云骑军的时候，是我的救命恩人。我仰慕他，也加入了云骑军，拿出十二分的努力表现，才加入了他所在的精英小队。
t_en: Actually, when Official Dahao was still a Cloud Knight, he saved my life. My admiration of him led me to join the Cloud Knights myself. I worked myself to the bone and eventually, I joined his elite squad.

t_ch: 但后来，景元将军担心他因为征战经历会提前引发魔阴身，便希望他来这长乐天做地衡司执事官，不必再经历生死别离的痛苦。
t_en: Later on, General Jing Yuan was worried about him getting struck with early-onset mara due to his combat experiences and hoped he could accept a sinecure position as a Realm-Keeping Commission official in the Exalting Sanctum — and keep him from the pains of life and death.

t_ch: 他这是来地衡司赋闲了？
t_en: So he came to the Realm-Keeping Commission to slack off?

t_ch: …嗯，我觉得话也不能这么说，毕竟地衡司的工作并不轻松。
t_en: ...Well, I don't think you can say that, as it's not exactly easy-going at the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 不过现在从结果上来看，他确实赋闲了，我却更忙了……
t_en: But judging by the results right now, he sure is idle. And I'm busier...

t_ch: 你为什么也在这里？
t_en: Why are you here too?

t_ch: 我向景元将军申请了调任，他允许我到地衡司里做一个小执事。
t_en: I applied for a transfer. General Jing Yuan let me come and be a minor officer at the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 我原本想能帮上大毫大人的忙就好，但是你看现在…我经常加班处理地衡司杂务到半夜，已经远远不止是「帮个忙」的程度了。
t_en: I thought it would be great to help out Official Dahao, but as you can now see... I'm always putting in overtime with chores until midnight, which is far more than just "doing a solid" for someone.

t_ch: 你说话有些太端着架子了。
t_en: You're behaving a little big for your britches.

t_ch: …现在是上班时间。您是前来咨询事务的化外民，我是地衡司的执事。严谨才是正确的地衡司执事工作态度。
t_en: ...It's business hours now. You're an Outworlder with an inquiry, and I am an officer of the Realm-Keeping Commission. Being disciplined is the expected work manner for a Realm-Keeping Commission officer.

t_ch: 难道说公职人员在上班时间应该跟您聊天开玩笑吗？
t_en: How would you feel if public officials crack jokes and chat with you during work hours?

t_ch: 你的同事们都挺随意的。
t_en: Your colleagues sure all love to.

t_ch: 我会批评他们的服务态度，感谢您对地衡司工作的关注和支持。
t_en: I will discipline them. Thank you for your attention and support to the work of the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 辛苦了！
t_en: Keep up the great work!

t_ch: 不辛苦，这是我们应该做的。感谢您对地衡司工作的关注和支持。
t_en: There's nothing great about it. It's the least that we could do. Thank you for your attention and support to the work of the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 我没有别的事情要咨询了。
t_en: I've nothing else to inquire.

t_ch: 好的。
t_en: Sure.

t_ch: 我没有别的事情要咨询了。
t_en: I've nothing else to inquire.

t_ch: 好的。
t_en: Sure.

t_ch: 请问地衡司有什么可以帮助你的吗？
t_en: Greetings. What can the Realm-Keeping Commission help you with today?

t_ch: 灾难过后你们还忙吗？
t_en: The disaster is over, are you guys still busy?

t_ch: 还可以吧。主要是不管有没有灾难，我都挺忙的…以至于现在也没什么灾难不灾难的实感。
t_en: I think the workload is okay. The thing is, I'm always busy, whether there is a disaster or not... I guess that's why I can't feel any difference.

t_ch: 我自己倒是无所谓，只是不希望大毫大人太忙了…你也知道，他那个岁数，那个身体，再忙容易忙出事。
t_en: It doesn't bother me, but I do hope that Official Dahao's schedule is less packed now... You know, considering his age and physical condition, it's easy for things to go wrong if he going hard at it.

t_ch: 但他自尊心又高，又不服老，我只能「偷」他的工作来做。我毕竟是持明，忙点就忙点吧，又不会忙到蜕生。
t_en: But he has a high self-esteem and refuses to accept that he's aged. So, I can only do some of his work for him secretly. As a Vidyadhara, this much work isn't going to force me into hatching rebirth.

t_ch: 哎！这事你可别跟他说啊，省得他又瞎操心。
t_en: Hey, please don't tell him about this. I don't want him to worry about me.

t_ch: 啊～这地衡司要是能直接把钱给我，让我不用出门，不用这么劳神费力地工作就好了。
t_en: Ah~ If the Realm-Keeping Commission could give me the cash directly and let me stay at home all day without doing this troublesome job, that'd be great.

t_ch: 反正不管我处理或不处理，就结果而言还是差不多的，不如去掉这些中间环节……
t_en: Anyway, it doesn't matter what I do or not do, the results are always gonna be the same. Why not cut out this middle part...

t_ch: 嗯？{F#好妹妹}{M#好兄弟}，偷听别人发牢骚可不是什么好习惯呀。怎么，有事吗？有大事要案的话请去找大毫大人，如果是其他的麻烦事儿，请去找净砚执事。
t_en: Hm? Eavesdropping is rude, {F#lady}{M#sir}, you know. What's up? If it's something important, please go to Official Dahao, and for literally anything else, please approach Officer Jingyan.

t_ch: 合着你什么事儿都不用做是吧？
t_en: So... You just don't deal with anything?

t_ch: 我是「地衡司内最聪明的头脑派」，只有他们两个都派不上用场的时候，我才会登场。换句话来说，我就像是「最后的王牌」，你在牌桌上会首先打出自己的王牌吗？
t_en: I'm the Realm-Keeping Commission's Most Intelligent, and I only show up when those two get stuck. In other words, I'm the Ultimate Trump Card. Do you lead with your ace when you're playing tiles?

t_ch: 这小小公廨里就只有三个执事…
t_en: There's only three officers staffing this tiny Chancery...

t_ch: 三个人怎么了嘛，三个人里也有可以有一位隐藏王牌嘛。
t_en: What about it? There can be a trump card hidden among three people.

t_ch: 这不是一本正经的胡说八道吗…
t_en: You're just spouting nonsense, aren't you?

t_ch: 三人行必有一人偷懒。只要我抢先偷懒，他们两个就没有机会偷懒了。
t_en: There's a saying. "Of three people, one will be a slacker." If I call dibs on slacking off, then the other two can't.

t_ch: 找你的话应该是什么事情？
t_en: What issues would you be handling normally?

t_ch: 我这个人比较怕麻烦，你要是有那种做起来不累也不麻烦的事情可以来找我。
t_en: I'm the kind of person who hates inconvenience. If you have any kind of work that's not too tiring, not too troublesome — then I'm down for that.

t_ch: 我是「地衡司内最聪明的头脑派」，只有他们两个都派不上用场的时候，我才会登场。你看他们俩，我主要指的是净砚执事，还在好好干活，那我就不用出手了。
t_en: I'm the Realm-Keeping Commission's Most Intelligent, and I only show up when those two get stuck. Just look at those two, and I'm mainly talking about the Officer Jingyan here — still going at it so hard, so I don't have to.

t_ch: 正好有一件麻烦事儿找你…
t_en: I just so happen to have a really thorny problem that I could use your help with...

t_ch: 别别别，你还是把麻烦事交给净砚吧，她才是地衡司的主心骨。
t_en: No no no, any thorny problem should be handed over to Jingyan. She's the main backbone of the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 你听说过一句话吗？「能力越小，责任越小」，我就是那个「小」。
t_en: Have you heard of this saying before? "With little power comes little responsibility." The little refers to me.

t_ch: 我现在就去告诉净砚执事。
t_en: I'm going to tell Officer Jingyan right now.

t_ch: 你去告诉她也没有用，那个人就是天生操劳命，她从来不放心我完成的事儿，每次都要亲自上手帮我解决「待处理的麻烦」。
t_en: Doesn't matter, mate. A person like her is born to work hard, and she never trusts what I do. Also, every time when there are "outstanding troubles" to deal with, she'll just complete it for me, personally.

t_ch: 所以你告诉她这些事情，只是在平添她的烦恼。我劝你还是不要这样做。
t_en: So telling her anything would just be adding to her troubles. I urge you not to do this.

t_ch: 哎呀，{F#好妹妹}{M#好兄弟}，你可别嫌弃我。别看我这样，我以前也是好好努力奋斗过的，好歹也算个总务处的精英执事。
t_en: *sigh* {F#Miss}{M#My man}, put aside your judgmental look. I've put in the hours working long and hard before, and I was, once upon a time, considered an elite officer of the General Affairs.

t_ch: 再说了，为什么我会在地衡司工作？要不是我投资失败…图表上所有的曲线往下走，只有物价还在往上走，不工作不行啊。
t_en: Besides, why do I work at the Realm-Keeping Commission? If it weren't for my failed investments... With all the curves on the chart going down, and only the prices are going up. Working has become a necessary option.

t_ch: 我努力过了，我失败了，现在这样就是我最好的工作状态了。
t_en: I hustled, and I failed. Now, this is the best effort I can put into work right now.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，你先别走，只要我们还在这闲聊，就不会有新的工作找上我。再聊一会儿吧，{F#好妹妹}{M#好兄弟}。
t_en: Hey, don't leave just yet. As long as we're chatting here, there won't be any new tasks assigned to me. Let's chat a little longer, {F#miss}{M#sir}.

t_ch: 那就聊一会儿吧。
t_en: Fine, let's chat for a while.

t_ch: 我可真是太喜欢你了，{F#好妹妹}{M#好兄弟}，我们随便聊点儿什么吧。
t_en: Thanks for doing that for me, {F#lady}{M#man}. Let's talk about literally anything.

t_ch: 地衡司里通常是做什么的？
t_en: So what do you actually get up to at the Realm-Keeping Commission?

t_ch: 据我观察，净砚基本什么都做。处理诈骗、聚众斗殴、各种突发事件处理，还有刑事案件的调查。「有困难找地衡司」，在罗浮仙舟上算是常识了。
t_en: Based on my observations, Jingyan basically does everything. Dealing with fraud, riots, various emergencies, and criminal investigations. "When there's trouble, call the Realm-Keeping Commission" really is a common saying about the Xianzhou Luofu.

t_ch: 怎么是「据我观察」呀？
t_en: What do you mean based on your observation?

t_ch: 这是我每天观察的结果，所以是「据我观察」。
t_en: That's just the conclusion I made from my day-to-day observations.

t_ch: 你做的都是什么？
t_en: What do you do, then?

t_ch: 「地衡司内最聪明的头脑派」当然是做一些需要头脑的工作了。
t_en: As the Realm-Keeping Commission's Most Intelligent, naturally I engage in cerebral activity.

t_ch: 我当然也会干活嘛，有些居民的猫猫狗狗、金人偃偶、或者是家用机巧走丢了的话，就轮到我上场了。我会申请外勤，然后到茶馆里去询问调查。
t_en: Of course I get some work done. Residents with lost cats, dogs, aurumaton puppets, or missing household ingenia — that's my wheelhouse. I would apply for field work, and then check out the teahouse to investigate.

t_ch: 听上去就很休闲。
t_en: Sounds very casual to me.

t_ch: 调查嘛，聊天也是调查，看看风景也是调查，找得到受人赞扬，找不到也没人怪罪我，我就喜欢这样的工作。
t_en: Well, you know, investigations. I like to think chatting is also one form of investigating. Admiring the scenery is an investigation too. When you solve a case, you're praised — but if you don't, nobody even blames you. This is the kind of job I like.

t_ch: 你难道没有责任心吗？
t_en: Don't you have any sense of duty?

t_ch: 你这话说的太严重了。我曾经也是一个很有责任心的执事，就是责任心太重，反倒是遭遇了打击…现在，我再也不敢承担责任了。
t_en: That's such a serious accusation. I used to be a very responsible officer too. In fact, it's because I was too responsible that I paid a heavy price... Now, I don't dare take on any more responsibility.

t_ch: 虽说我在公廨里的地位连一只小谛听都不如，但是我从不气馁，只要我不争气，就没有什么可以生气了。
t_en: I might be lower in the Chancery's pecking order than even Diting, but I never lose courage. As long as I never try to live up to expectations, there's nothing to get mad about.

t_ch: 你如果听说了有什么帮富家小姐姐成天遛狗的工作，记得告诉我，我可以随时辞掉地衡司里的工作。
t_en: If you hear about a rich lady who needs her dog walked all day, let me know. I can quit my job at the Realm-Keeping Commission any time.

t_ch: 这个地衡司公廨好小呀。
t_en: This Realm-Keeping Commission Chancery sure is a small place.

t_ch: 毕竟，我们只负责一个区域嘛。
t_en: After all, we only look after one zone.

t_ch: 地衡司是仙舟罗浮的庶务管理机构，也是罗浮老百姓接触机会最多的机构。为了方便管理、服务于民，不同地区都有各自的地衡司公廨。
t_en: The Realm-Keeping Commission is the general affairs organization of the Xianzhou Luofu, and also the institution that engages with the public of Luofu most often. Every zone has its own Realm-Keeping Commission Chancery to keep up with the needs of citizens.

t_ch: 罗浮仙舟上还有很多其他的公廨，论大小来看都挺小的，贵在我们数量多嘛。
t_en: There are many other Chanceries aboard the Xianzhou Luofu, and they're all pretty small in size, but large in amount.

t_ch: 你为什么还没有被开除…
t_en: How have you not been fired yet...

t_ch: 话也不用说得这么绝嘛。我这人虽然干活不勤快，但是我没有犯下什么大错，还不至于被开除吧。
t_en: There's no need to be like that. I may not be diligent in my work, but I haven't committed any severe fireable offenses yet.

t_ch: 我这么说吧，就算是那位「全能执事」净砚大人，也常常因为不够圆滑，与前来办事的老百姓发生冲突，而这个时候只要让我登场，事情就会好办许多。
t_en: Let me put it this way. Even Almighty Officer Jingyan has clashed with people who came for official business because she's not tactful enough. And that's where I come in, and things will go more smoothly.

t_ch: 齿轮有齿轮的用法，链条有链条的用法，这才是职场里的「各司其职」嘛。
t_en: Everyone has their place in a place like this. Just let the gears turn as they do, and the chains pull as they do.

t_ch: 我倒是听一些异星游客聊起家乡的职场文化，像我这样的工作态度早就被赶出地衡司了。哎，真是不得不感慨，还是罗浮仙舟好呀。
t_en: I have heard some Outworlders talking about workplace culture back in their homeworld. Work attitudes like mine would have long been driven out of their version of Realm-Keeping Commissions. Ah, I do proclaim the Xianzhou Luofu to be a far superior place.

t_ch: 就闲聊到这儿吧。
t_en: That's enough chit-chat, then.

t_ch: 好呀，有事儿的时候去找净砚，没有事儿的时候来找我就行。
t_en: Alright, if you have problems need solving, trouble Jingyan. And if you have time need killing, you're welcome to chat with me.

t_ch: 就闲聊到这儿吧。
t_en: That's enough chit-chat, then.

t_ch: 好呀，有事儿的时候去找净砚，没有事儿的时候来找我就行。
t_en: Alright, if you have problems need solving, trouble Jingyan. And if you have time need killing, you're welcome to chat with me.

t_ch: 最近长乐天不是流行起推理小说了嘛，「安乐椅侦探」无须奔波劳碌，坐在躺椅上听着命案的线索就能找出真凶…我也想做这样的「安乐椅执事」。
t_en: Mystery novels are really popular around the Exalting Sanctum these days, right? Those armchair detectives crack cases just by sitting on their couch and following the story... I'd love to be an 'armchair officer' like them!

t_ch: {F#好妹妹}{M#好兄弟}，找我是有什么想聊的吗？工作免谈啊。
t_en: How's it going, {F#miss}{M#man}? Got anything you'd like to talk about? Anything but work!

t_ch: 灾难过后你们还忙吗？
t_en: The disaster is over, are you guys still busy?

t_ch: 说实话，感觉比罗浮最乱的时候还忙…之前我也没想通，怎么事情结束了，我反而越来越忙了……
t_en: To be honest, it feels even busier than when things were at their most chaotic at the Luofu. I don't get it. How is it that after everything is over, I only find myself getting busier and busier...

t_ch: 后来我想通了，主要是因为灾难结束了以后，净砚和大毫两位大人腾得出手来收拾我了。
t_en: Later on, I found out the reason. It was because Jingyan and Dahao were free enough to deal with me after the disaster ended.

t_ch: 之前还能趁乱摸摸鱼，现在不得不干点活咯…哎呀呀，头大，头大。
t_en: Before, I could take advantage of the chaos and slack off a bit, but I actually have to do work now... Ugh, this is giving me a headache.

t_ch: 哎，竟然还有人愿意和我这么个老头子说话…咳咳…人老了以后喝口水都容易呛成肺炎。我这老化的气管…
t_en: Oh, so there is someone still willing to chat with this old coffin dodger... Ahem... When people get older, they can catch pneumonia just by having a sip of water. My aching aging throat...

t_ch: 您没事儿吧。
t_en: Are you alright?

t_ch: 呵呵，他们给我吃过药了，慢慢休养就没事了……
t_en: Haha, they've already given me meds, so I'll just rest and recover...

t_ch: 我看您身体硬朗，不至于吧…
t_en: Your body looks healthy. What's all this about...

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}你也太乐观了，我未必还活得到痊愈那天，哎…咳咳……
t_en: Dear {F#girl}{M#boy}, you're far too optimistic. I may not even survive to see a complete recovery... *cough*...

t_ch: 你是谁啊？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 我叫白图泰，以前的本职是克里姆特立宪国的法官，副业是旅行作家…不过，现在只是个等待天舶司遣返的犯罪分子罢了……
t_en: I'm Battuta. I used to be a judge in the Klimt Republic. My side job was a travel writer... But now, I'm just a criminal waiting around for the Sky-Faring Commission to repatriate me...

t_ch: 你为什么在这儿？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 等待天舶司遣返的犯罪分子在这儿很奇怪吗？我叫白图泰…以前的本职是克里姆特立宪国的法官，副业是旅行作家……
t_en: Is it really strange for criminals waiting for repatriation by the Sky-Faring Commission to be waiting here? I'm Battuta. I used to be a judge in the Klimt Republic. My side hustle was a travel writer...

t_ch: 看在你这么关心我一个坏老头子的份上，给你一个建议——年轻人，不要妄图刺探那些不该知道的东西，这仙舟的水太深，你把握不住……
t_en: Just because you cared for an old criminal geezer like me, here's a piece of advice for ya — kid, don't spy on things you shouldn't know about. The water of Xianzhou runs deep, you won't be able to handle it...

t_ch: 怎么，你还需要罪人的建议吗？
t_en: Huh, still looking for the advice of a criminal?

t_ch: 想陪你聊聊天…
t_en: I just want to chat with you...

t_ch: 哈哈哈…咳咳…我本以为现在人心不古…那就聊聊吧，也许你能从我那「短暂」的生命中学到什么吧……
t_en: Hahaha... *cough*... I thought people nowadays are just a bunch of heartless savages... Let's talk, maybe you can learn something from my short life...

t_ch: 你来自什么样的国家呢？
t_en: What kind of country do you come from?

t_ch: 我的国家啊…克里姆特立宪国，一个很好的国家…咳咳…我们有广袤的疆域、高效的行政系统、清廉的官员、正直的人民……
t_en: My homeland... The Klimt Republic is a great nation... *cough*... We have a vast territory, an efficient government, honest officials, and upstanding people...

t_ch: 克里姆特立宪国可说是由伟大的「开拓」阿基维利一手促成，祂开辟星轨，引领祖先穿过流放苦旅，抵达长夏乐土。
t_en: The Klimt Republic was said to be made from nothing by Akivili the Trailblazer when THEY established the star rail, leading my ancestors on THEIR path of exile to THEIR Eversummer Paradise.

t_ch: 为感念祂的恩德，先祖们遴选勇士，踏上漫长的开拓之旅，将一个个死寂星球改造成宜居世界……
t_en: In gratitude for THEIR kindness, my ancestors selected warriors and embarked on trailblazing expeditions to transform dead worlds into living ones...

t_ch: 立国千年…咳咳…我们已经拥有了十七个恒星系，幅员辽阔、物产丰茂…所以，国家需要我这种人——「法官」。
t_en: About a millennium has passed since its foundation... *cough*... We've already seen seventeen star systems, with a vast territory, and rich products... That's why our nation needs someone like me: A Judge.

t_ch: 很多行星的生态环境与我们的首都迥然相异…所以宰相和议会…咳咳…也就是我们的政府，需要我这样的法官去帮助各地制定和修订因地制宜的法律……
t_en: Many planets exist in ecological environments different from this one... And so the prime minister and council... ahem... That is, our government needs judges like me to help ratify laws and regulations tailored to each of the differing local conditions...

t_ch: 咳咳…二十岁那年，我从皇家法学院提前毕业，二十四岁就成为了法学博士…一年后就考下了法官的资格证……
t_en: *cough*... When I was <unbreak>20</unbreak>, I graduated from the Royal Academy of Laws, becoming a Doctor of Law at <unbreak>24</unbreak>... A year later, I passed the qualification examination to become a judge...

t_ch: 我还记得…时任宰相维森纳女士，亲自授予了我缎带，鼓励我像她一样，为祖国的人民奉献终身……
t_en: I can still remember... The prime minister at the time, Madam Wiesener, awarding me my medal and encouraging me to dedicate my life to the people of the motherland, just like her...

t_ch: 啊…那时候我多么有活力啊…维森纳女士为我戴上缎带的那一刻，我觉得自己像一个雀跃的小伙子一样，永远也不会疲惫……
t_en: Ahhh... How energized I was back then... The moment Madam Wiesener placed that medal on me, I felt like a carefree kid again — never to be tired again...

t_ch: 那种充满活力的幻觉是如此强烈，以至于二十年前，我去参加维森纳女士的国葬时…才第一次意识到，这一切也终会发生在我身上……
t_en: That fantasy of vitality was so strong that it was only twenty years ago, when attending the state funeral of Madam Wiesener... That I realized for the first time that all of this would happen to me one day...

t_ch: 衰老到没办法处理公务、虚弱到挺不过连续的星际航行，最后无助地躺在病床上，绝望地细数自己有多少应做却未做的，又有多少应见却未见的……
t_en: Too old to handle official duties, too weak to handle continual interstellar travel, I ultimately lie here helpless on a hospital bed, desperately counting all the things I should've done but didn't, all those people I should've seen but hadn't...

t_ch: 呵呵…这最终使我变成了自己最讨厌的人，忠诚于欲望不惜破坏法律的人……
t_en: Hehe... That has twisted me into the kind of person I hate most, the kind who's loyal to their desires and gives no second thoughts to breaking the law...

t_ch: 你提到过自己是旅行作家？
t_en: You mentioned that you used to be a travel writer?

t_ch: 我曾是个旅行作家…咳咳…来到仙舟之前，我走遍了祖国广袤的疆域，体验不同恒星系的风土人情。
t_en: I used to be a travelog writer... *cough*... Before coming to Xianzhou, I'd traveled all over the cosmos, experiencing all the customs and peoples of different star systems.

t_ch: 我将这些旅行体验如实地记录下来，付梓出版，随即引起了热烈的反响……
t_en: I recorded these travel experiences in truth, put them into print, and immediately aroused enthusiastic responses...

t_ch: 其实我很清楚…咳咳…这并非因为我的文字多么秀美壮丽，而是因为克里姆特原本就是如此…只要如实记录，就足以打动读者。
t_en: Actually, I have no misgivings... ahem... It's not due to any beautiful prose of mine, but rather because Klimt has always been like that... Any non-fiction writing is enough to rouse an impressed readership.

t_ch: 先代宰相维森纳女士有一句名言：「谁用双脚丈量过祖国的土地，谁就愿将鲜血撒在这里。」她所言非虚…咳咳……
t_en: The late Madam Wiesener, once said, "Whosoever has measured the soils of the motherland with their feet, is willing to spill their blood there." What she said is true... *cough*...

t_ch: 我曾在最富庶的行星上目睹新的城市拔地而起…我也曾在最贫瘠的行星上与牧民一起劳作…我见过祖国每一颗行星的日出日落，也见过每一片大海的潮涨潮消……
t_en: I've seen new cities rise up on the richest planets... I've worked with nomadic herders on the poorest planets... I've seen the sun rise or set on every planet in my home system, and also the ebb and flow of every tide...

t_ch: 在灾害横行的地方，我与当地的农夫一起对抗兽灾…在边境星域，我与戍边战士们一起从反物质军团手中幸存……
t_en: In places where disasters rage rampant, I've fought against the local beasts with the farmers. On frontier segmenta, I've somehow survived combat against the Antimatter Legion...

t_ch: 可我所见到的却仍然只是沧海一粟…咳咳…我的旅途还未结束…如今却即将带着遗憾死去…我贪婪长生？没错，不过我更贪婪旅行者的使命。
t_en: But all I've seen is still but a drop in the ocean... *cough*... My journey is not yet complete... But I will die soon with regrets... Am I greedy for longevity? Yes, but I'm greedier for the calling of a traveler.

t_ch: …都结束了，结束了。
t_en: ...It's over, it's all over.

t_ch: 所以你为了长生来到罗浮？
t_en: And so you came to Luofu to experience longevity?

t_ch: 真是个敏锐的小家伙…不过还是让我从头说起吧。
t_en: You really are a quick one, aren't you... But please, let me start at the beginning...

t_ch: 大概三十岁出头的时候吧…有一次我去见先代宰相维森纳女士…我和她说，我准备参选宰相……
t_en: When I was in my early thirties... I once went to see the late Madam Wiesener... I told her I was going to run for prime ministry...

t_ch: 那时候我还年轻，充满了热情…但维森纳女士摇了摇头，对我说：「还太早，你先到人间去吧。」
t_en: I was still young back then, and brimming with exuberance... But Madam Wiesener shook her head and told me, "It's still early. Go to the human world, first."

t_ch: 然后我去了「人间」…看到边境的战地医院缺医少药，看到沙漠中的农民艰难求生，看到不是每个女孩经历着「公平的人生」……
t_en: And then I went to the "human world"... And saw the field hospitals on the frontlines lacking doctors and medicine. I saw the farmers in the desert struggling to survive. I saw that not every young girl out there got to experience "a fair life"...

t_ch: 那些岁月里我留下了很多承诺，并为此奋战至今…然后我大梦初醒般地发现，自己已经再也走不动了……
t_en: I made a lot of promises back in those days, and I've been fighting to keep them to this day... But then I woke up from a long dream only to discover that I could no longer walk...

t_ch: 等你上了年纪，你就会发觉自己的身体像一座出了毛病的老座钟…开始你以为只是少上了油，接下来齿轮磨耗、发条松脱，摆子都打不准了…咳咳……
t_en: Wait until you're old, and you'll find that your body is like an old grandfather clock that's gone wrong... At first, you think it just needs some oil, then the gears wear out, the mainspring loosens, and the pendulum loses its accuracy... *cough*...

t_ch: 人就像骑着独轮车，同时还在表演接抛球的猴子似的，手忙脚乱地想接连兜住空中掉落的三个球，还得脚踩车轮，维持平衡……
t_en: People are just like monkeys riding a unicycle while trying to juggle. While hurrying to catch those three balls up in the air, you still have to keep stepping on those pedals to spin the wheel and maintain balance...

t_ch: 我老了，多少也有些糊涂了，但每一个承诺都记得很清楚…恐怕再也没有时间去实现它们了……
t_en: I'm old, and somewhat confused, but I remember every promise well... I'm afraid I'll never have time to fulfill them...

t_ch: 这就是为什么我要来仙舟，我必须来这里…这里有抵抗时间的唯一办法。
t_en: That's why I've come to Xianzhou, because I have to be here... This is the only place where I can fight the ravages of time.

t_ch: 我该走了…
t_en: I should go...

t_ch: 嗯…你走吧，别把时间浪费在一个罪人这里。
t_en: Mmm, you go then. Don't waste your time here any longer with a criminal.

t_ch: 我该走了…
t_en: I should go...

t_ch: 嗯…你走吧，快些走，别把时间浪费在一个罪人这里。
t_en: Mmm, you go then, quickly. Don't waste your time here any longer with a criminal.

t_ch: 你又来啦？虽然…咳咳…虽然有人能陪我聊几句，我心里还是挺舒服的。说吧…找我还有什么事？
t_en: Are you back? Even though... *cough*... Even though you can only join me for a few moments, I still feel great about it. Tell me, to what do I owe the honor?

t_ch: 请问大人是有事要进入神策府吗？我去帮大人通报一下。
t_en: May I ask, have you come here to the Seat of Divine Foresight on any official business? I can help you pass it on.

t_ch: 我要进入神策府。
t_en: I wanna enter the Seat of Divine Foresight.

t_ch: 大人在此稍作等候，在下这就前去通报。
t_en: Could you please wait here a moment? I'll go and report in for you.

t_ch: 不用，我只是路过而已。
t_en: Nah, I'm just passing by.

t_ch: 您好，尊敬的异邦人。我是翔水商会的专员，我叫茂贞。请问您也是来此处洽谈商业合作的域外行商吗？
t_en: Hello, dear traveler. I am Maozhen, a specialist from the Aquaglider Guild. Are you an Outworld traveling merchant also here to discuss some business opportunities?

t_ch: 等一下，我这仔细一看才看出来…您是{NICKNAME}？嘿，您看看我这记性，竟然忘了咱们流云渡的大英雄！
t_en: Wait a minute. Let me take a closer look... You're {NICKNAME}? Hey, I somehow forgot who the great hero of Cloudford was!

t_ch: 哎呀，真不好意思。这流云渡是一处商港，往来起落的也都是货轮，我就先入为主地以为您也是位域外行商了。
t_en: Ack, I'm really sorry. Cloudford is a commercial port, and the only ships that visit are freighters, so I presumed that you were an outworld traveling merchant.

t_ch: 若不是您协助罗浮解决了星核危机，这些在流云渡讨生活的人…仙舟人也好异邦人也罢，有一个算一个都要饿死的。
t_en: If it weren't for you helping the Luofu with the Stellaron Crisis, all the people coming to Cloudford to seek a living... be they Xianzhou people or travelers, they'd have all starved to death.

t_ch: 请您收下这些谢礼吧！
t_en: Please, accept this as thanks!

t_ch: 这也太客气了…
t_en: You're too kind...

t_ch: 您这是哪儿的话！这小小薄礼，可实在难以传达我对您的感激与崇敬啊……
t_en: What are you talking about! It's just a small gift to convey my gratitude and admiration...

t_ch: 这是谁的邮包？
t_en: Whose parcel is this?

t_ch: 这是囤积在流云渡逾期未取的邮包。一般来说，这种逾期未取的邮包我们是要拍卖掉的，但您毕竟是流云渡的英雄，这些邮包我可以直接低价卖给您。
t_en: This is an overdue parcel in the backlog at Cloudford. Usually we auction the abandoned ones off, but you're the hero of Cloudford after all, so why don't I cut you a deal and sell it to you directly for a low price?

t_ch: 这次这个就当做是谢礼送给您了，如果想要试试手气，可以再来找我。我这里囤积了很多呢。
t_en: This time, I'll gift it to you as thanks. If you ever wanna try your luck, come find me again. I have stocked up quite the backlog.

t_ch: 请问您还有其他事情吗？
t_en: Is there anything else I can help with?

t_ch: 想问你点事。
t_en: I'd like to ask you something.

t_ch: 您请问，我一定有问必答。
t_en: Ask away, and I'll answer anything I can.

t_ch: 你是做什么的？
t_en: What do you do?

t_ch: 我是天舶司翔水商会派驻在流云渡的专员，负责接洽来到罗浮进行商业贸易的域外行商。
t_en: I'm a representative of the Aquaglider Guild sent to Cloudford by the Sky-Faring Commission to handle all Outworld traveling merchants who come and trade on the Luofu.

t_ch: 我们翔水商会是天舶司麾下七大商会之一，您要是有大宗商品想要出手，欢迎随时来找我。
t_en: Our Aquaglider Guild is one of the seven guilds under the umbrella of the Sky-Faring Commission. If you have any bulk goods that you wish to sell off, come see me any time.

t_ch: 别的地方我管不到，但在这流云渡我说话还是有点分量的。您要是有什么需要，我一定优先为您处理。
t_en: I'm no use elsewhere, but at least here in Cloudford my words still hold some weight. If there's anything you need, you'll be my top priority.

t_ch: 最近有什么烦恼吗？
t_en: Any trouble going on recently?

t_ch: 唉，怎么说呢…最近的烦恼比之前少多了，但还是有不少的。
t_en: Hmm, how do I put it... There's less trouble than before, but it's still there.

t_ch: 之前建木生发，罗浮的商业活动几乎完全停摆了…这可苦了我们这些在流云渡上讨生活的人。
t_en: With the Ambrosial Arbor's new growth, business on the Luofu almost shut down completely before... Which is terrible for those of us trying to earn a living out here in Cloudford.

t_ch: 我好歹是做官的，有固定工资。可那些商人和工人，那真是完全断了生计来源，苦不堪言啊。
t_en: I'm lucky to be an official and have a fixed salary. But those merchants and workers have been completely cut off from their livelihood, and they're miserable.

t_ch: 现如今虽然在您的帮助下解决了危机，可危机的余波却迟迟没有消退……
t_en: And now, despite your help with resolving the crisis, its aftermath continues to rage on...

t_ch: 建木生发导致的阴阳相感让机巧鸟到处乱飞，丢了好几十只…处理出入港系统的术算机也坏掉了，造成了好几场交通事故……
t_en: The Ambrosial Arbor's new growth set the cycranes flying about all over the place, and we lost dozens... The calculation device processing entry and exit at the port also broke down, causing several traffic accidents...

t_ch: 不过，一切终归还是在朝着好的方向发展。我相信很快，一切都会变好的，而您对此居功至伟。
t_en: But everything is still moving along in the right direction. I'm sure that things will get better soon, and you can take credit for most of that.

t_ch: 关于辛克莱和麦尔彦…
t_en: About Sinclair and Maryam...

t_ch: 那两个商人啊…他们俩常年在罗浮做进出口贸易，想要贸易的货物重叠度比较高，所以关系相当紧张。
t_en: Those two merchants... They've been trading on the Luofu for years, and they often trade in the same stuff, so their relationship is pretty tense.

t_ch: 他们现在手里都积压着不少赛伯邑超导合金，想要卖到罗浮…虽然这超导合金颇为珍贵，但只在工造司生产的一些精密仪器中应用，需求量很小。
t_en: They've been stockpiling lots of Cybercity superconducting alloys and plan on selling them off to the Luofu... It might be valuable, but it's only used in a few hyper-precision instruments over at the Artisanship Commission, so there's little demand for it.

t_ch: 所以该从谁手上购入这批超导合金，就成了我该头疼的问题。
t_en: So the question of who to buy these superconducting alloys from has grown into a rather thorny problem for me.

t_ch: 不过嘛…让他们俩打一打倒也是好事。每次他们俩陷入这种竞争，两人的报价都会一降再降。
t_en: Nevertheless... It's great that there's competition between the two. Every time the two of them get into this kind of trade war, their prices drop like crazy.

t_ch: 我再煽风点火一下，比如「我听说最近有其他商团也有超导合金想要出手」之类的…他们的价格还能再降一些。
t_en: Let me stir things up a bit more, something like, "I heard there's another guild selling superconducting alloys these days"... Their price might drop yet.

t_ch: 所以我决定再观望一下，让他俩再压一压价格。反正也不是第一次了，他们俩也该习惯了，嘿嘿。
t_en: So I've decided to wait and see. Let them push down their prices again. In any case, this isn't the first time, and they should get used to it because it won't be the last!

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 好嘞，那我们下次见。不要忘记之后来我这里买一些过期货物哦～
t_en: Alright, see you next time. Don't forget to come by later and buy up some of these abandoned goods.

t_ch: 我想买个过期邮包试试手气。
t_en: I'd like to buy an abandoned parcel to try my luck.

t_ch: 好说。我这里囤积的过期货物可不少呢～希望您这次运气不错。
t_en: Not a problem. I've got loads of abandoned goods here~ Hope you strike it lucky this time.

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 好嘞，那我们下次见。不要忘记之后来我这里买一些过期货物哦～
t_en: Alright, see you next time. Don't forget to come by later and buy up some of these abandoned goods.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}英雄！您又来了～有什么事要办吗？还是想来买个逾期处理的邮包来试试手气？
t_en: {NICKNAME} the Hero! You're back~ What business brings you here? Or have you come looking to try your luck with an abandoned parcel?

t_ch: 想问你点事。
t_en: I'd like to ask you something.

t_ch: 您请问，我一定有问必答。
t_en: Ask away, and I'll answer anything I can.

t_ch: 你是做什么的？

t_en: Alright, see you next time. Don't forget to come by later and buy up some of these abandoned goods.

t_ch: 老家贸易不发达，商会批文怎么还不下？你你你，就是你，你是怎么回事？
t_en: My home planet doesn't have much trade as it is. Why haven't the guild permits been released yet? Y—You, yeah you! What are you doing?

t_ch: 你要是也在等天舶司的文件，就必须排到我后面去，去去去，别在这里碍事情。
t_en: If you're awaiting documents from the Sky-Faring Commission, you'd better get behind me in line. Go on, then — get out of the way.
